nodes.displayEquation: d tau / dt = alpha on the frozen centerline branch
nodes.plainMeaning: Lapse field controlling the relation between coordinate time and normal-observer proper time in the selected 3+1 chart.
nodes.whyItMatters: This is the whitepaper's cleanest observable for separating clocking from unsupported speed language.

figurePlan.purpose: Show alpha and alpha - 1 with field statistics and centerline sampling.

inputs.name: alpha
inputs.meaning: lapse samples from the metric brick

defaultAxisTemplates.captionTemplate: 3+1 observable: lapse field alpha sampled along the NHM2 centerline.
defaultAxisTemplates.uncertaintyTemplate: Values are repo-normalized diagnostic samples; proper-time interpretation depends on the declared observer assumptions.

nodes.displayEquation: dx^i + beta^i dt enters the 3+1 line element
nodes.plainMeaning: Shift vector describing spatial coordinate drift between hypersurfaces in the declared chart.
nodes.whyItMatters: The map keeps beta as a chart-dependent transport descriptor rather than an ordinary ship speed.

figurePlan.purpose: Show beta_x and |beta| with independent dynamic ranges.

inputs.name: beta_x
inputs.meaning: x component of shift from the metric brick

defaultAxisTemplates.captionTemplate: 3+1 observable: shift component and magnitude along the NHM2 centerline.
defaultAxisTemplates.uncertaintyTemplate: Shift is a chart-dependent descriptor, not a direct speed claim.

nodes.plainMeaning: Spatial metric on each 3+1 hypersurface.
nodes.whyItMatters: Observer normalization and stress projections depend on the same-chart spatial basis.

figurePlan.purpose: Show which spatial-metric components are available to downstream observer and source routes.

nodes.plainMeaning: Embedding/evolution geometry of the spatial hypersurfaces, including trace-like or expansion diagnostics used by the repo.
nodes.whyItMatters: This is the bridge from visual theta/low-expansion diagnostics to the actual 3+1 geometry grammar.

figurePlan.purpose: Show signed theta or trace-like diagnostics with zero-centered scaling and near-zero metadata.

nodes.plainMeaning: Signed-distance field defining hull, wall, and exterior-shell region masks.
nodes.whyItMatters: Regional closure claims require whole-region masks rather than selected patches on the hull.

figurePlan.purpose: Show nested hull, wall, and exterior-shell region masks used for comparisons.

nodes.plainMeaning: Stress-energy tensor required by the solved metric in the selected chart and basis.
nodes.whyItMatters: This is the authoritative metric-required side of the source-closure comparison.

figurePlan.purpose: Show required tensor components and same-chart basis status.

nodes.plainMeaning: Metric-required tensor aggregated over hull, wall, and exterior_shell masks.
nodes.whyItMatters: Global agreement is not enough; the closure problem is regional and same-basis.

figurePlan.purpose: Show metric-required tensor summaries by region.

nodes.plainMeaning: Unit normal observer field used for same-foliation projection.
nodes.whyItMatters: This makes the observer real and chart-declared rather than a view-from-nowhere summary.

figurePlan.purpose: Show the observer path and region bands used for projection audits.

nodes.plainMeaning: Energy density measured by the selected observer family.
nodes.whyItMatters: Energy-condition claims must be observer-family claims, not single coordinate-component claims.

figurePlan.purpose: Show observer energy density along the centerline with region bands.

nodes.plainMeaning: Observer-projected momentum density.
nodes.whyItMatters: A diagonal-only stress proxy cannot substitute for full observer momentum channels.

figurePlan.purpose: Show J_i component coverage and selected profiles.

nodes.plainMeaning: Spatial stress projected into the hypersurface.
nodes.whyItMatters: Full/off-diagonal stress authority is one of the central closure pressure points.

figurePlan.purpose: Show spatial-stress component authority and missing/review-gated cells.

forbiddenClaims: no full tensor promotion without evidence

nodes.plainMeaning: Fabrication/layout-scale source element and idealized Casimir baseline, not a direct spacetime field.
nodes.whyItMatters: This separates real Casimir context from an unsupported macro-curvature bridge.

figurePlan.purpose: Show representative tile layers as fabrication/process geometry only.

inputs.name: A
inputs.meaning: tile plate area in square meters

defaultAxisTemplates.captionTemplate: Source-model exploration: idealized static Casimir tile energy versus declared gap.
defaultAxisTemplates.uncertaintyTemplate: This is an idealized source-model sweep and does not establish a macro-curvature source bridge.

forbiddenClaims: no macro-curvature validation from Casimir context

nodes.plainMeaning: Scheduled sector activation model from the NHM2 cavity contract.
nodes.whyItMatters: Duty-cycle figures should show schedule structure, not imply active energy intensity.

figurePlan.purpose: Show every scheduled sector and the concurrent-sector count from the contract.

nodes.plainMeaning: Proposed source-side tensor counterpart in the same chart, units, region mask, aggregation mode, and normalization basis.
nodes.whyItMatters: This is the missing or review-gated bridge needed before mechanism-side language can become stronger.

figurePlan.purpose: Show source-side counterpart tensor coverage and review-gated components.

nodes.plainMeaning: Same-chart, same-basis closure residual between metric-required and tile-effective tensors.
nodes.whyItMatters: This is the core mathematical gap between the classical metric solve and the quantum/source-side route.

figurePlan.purpose: Show closure residuals only where both tensors are available in the same basis.

outputs.name: deltaT
outputs.meaning: same-basis residual where counterpart data exists

defaultAxisTemplates.captionTemplate: Closure residual: Delta T_ab shown only where both metric-required and tile-effective values are available.
defaultAxisTemplates.uncertaintyTemplate: Missing or review-gated components are status-coded, not plotted as zero.

forbiddenClaims: no closure promotion while regional basis is diagnostic

nodes.plainMeaning: Regional source-closure status by whole-hull region.
nodes.whyItMatters: This is where the atlas should show what remains unresolved rather than painting blocker status on the hull.

figurePlan.purpose: Show hull, wall, and exterior_shell residual or blocker status as a chart.

nodes.plainMeaning: Observer-family energy-condition diagnostics for the stress-energy tensor.
nodes.whyItMatters: Energy-condition panels must show observer-family evidence and missing families, not binary optimism.

figurePlan.purpose: Show which observer families and conditions are evidenced, missing, or review-gated.

nodes.symbol: int f(tau) T_mn u^m u^n d tau
nodes.displayEquation: integral of sampled stress-energy along a worldline
nodes.plainMeaning: Weighted worldline sampling requirement for QEI-style audit.
nodes.whyItMatters: This keeps QEI as a mathematical dossier requirement, not a decorative pass badge.

figurePlan.purpose: Show sampling window, worldline, and dossier completeness as pending/review evidence.

defaultAxisTemplates.captionTemplate: QEI sampling requirement: weighted worldline window shown as an audit requirement.
defaultAxisTemplates.uncertaintyTemplate: This is a sampling-window requirement visualization, not a completed QEI bound.

nodes.plainMeaning: Run reproducibility and convergence evidence for the map-to-figure chain and full solve.
nodes.whyItMatters: A figure is not acceptance evidence unless its source run, hashes, and convergence status are auditable.

figurePlan.purpose: Show convergence/reproduction fields, hashes, and missing evidence.

forbiddenClaims: no reproducibility promotion without full chain

nodes.plainMeaning: Dimensionless regional curvature leverage for the NHM2 full solve, computed from the metric-required tensor route and the declared region scale.
nodes.whyItMatters: This connects scale-normalized curvature reasoning to the full-solve tensor path without promoting mechanism-side source closure.

figurePlan.purpose: Show Lambda_R by region beside tensor source refs, residual status, and blocked closure gates.

nodes.plainMeaning: Frozen-run claim boundary for validation, physical-mechanism, and promotion language.
nodes.whyItMatters: This keeps the observable-equation atlas from turning context, diagnostics, or literature into promotion.

figurePlan.purpose: Show the three locked claim fields and their non-promotional meaning.

edges.plainMeaning: The lapse contributes directly to the Eulerian normal observer.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: This defines observer bookkeeping only; it does not validate transport.

edges.plainMeaning: The shift enters n^mu through -beta^i / alpha.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: This is chart geometry, not speed certification.

edges.plainMeaning: The spatial metric and projector define observer-facing energy variables.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Projection does not imply energy-condition clearance.

edges.plainMeaning: Metric-derived tensor routes depend on the 3+1 geometry state.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Metric-required source is not source-side mechanism evidence.

edges.plainMeaning: Region masks define hull, wall, and exterior-shell aggregation.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Region masks must cover whole regions rather than selected decorative patches.

edges.plainMeaning: The metric-required tensor is one side of the closure residual.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Restating G_ab/8pi on both sides is not source closure.

edges.plainMeaning: The tile-effective tensor is the required source-side counterpart.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Unavailable or review-gated components block stronger mechanism language.

edges.plainMeaning: Regional closure requires region-specific metric-required tensors.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Global residuals cannot substitute for region-resolved closure.

edges.plainMeaning: The metric-required tensor route supplies the curvature norm used by NHM2 regional leverage.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: This tensor-first leverage is diagnostic and does not confirm a source mechanism.

edges.plainMeaning: Region masks and lever lengths define the scale factor in Lambda_R.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Scale-normalization cannot replace same-basis regional closure.

edges.plainMeaning: Curvature leverage records the regional tensor scale beside the closure residual.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Lambda_R is a diagnostic tensor measure, not a closure verdict.

edges.plainMeaning: Tile layout informs the source model but does not directly produce macro tensor authority.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Casimir context remains source-model context until a same-basis tensor is derived.

edges.plainMeaning: Sector scheduling can support a duty-averaged source model only under explicit averaging assumptions.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Duty scheduling is not a certified thrust or source law.

edges.plainMeaning: Observer energy density feeds WEC/NEC/SEC/DEC diagnostics.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: A projected scalar does not clear all observer-family conditions.

edges.plainMeaning: Momentum density is part of full observer stress-energy authority.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Diagonal-only evidence blocks promotion-sensitive energy-condition language.

edges.plainMeaning: Spatial stress components contribute to observer-family condition tests.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Off-diagonal review status must remain visible.

edges.plainMeaning: QEI-style audits require weighted stress-energy sampling along worldlines.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: A sampling schematic is not a completed QEI bound.

edges.plainMeaning: Review-gated or missing closure keeps claim locks active.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Locks remain false until ledger evidence explicitly changes them.

edges.plainMeaning: Missing QEI/QFT dossier evidence blocks physical-mechanism language.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: External QEI literature constrains the route but does not complete it.

edges.plainMeaning: Reproducibility and convergence evidence document whether figures can be treated as acceptance evidence.
edges.claimBoundaryNote: Figure generation alone cannot promote a run.
